findings.detail: Dangerous SUID binary found: /usr/bin/mount
findings.evidence: mount can be abused to mount attacker-controlled filesystems
findings.remediation: Remove SUID bit: chmod u-s /usr/bin/mount (if not required)

findings.detail: Total: 11 SUID binaries, 4 SGID binaries found on system
findings.evidence: Full enumeration complete

findings.detail: Kernel version: 4.4.0 | Architecture: amd64

findings.detail: [CVE-2022-0847] DirtyPipe - Overwrite data in arbitrary read-only files, leading to privilege escalation
findings.evidence: Affected versions: 5.8 to 5.16.10 | Current: 4.4.0 | VERIFY MANUALLY
findings.remediation: Update kernel to latest patched version. Check: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2022-0847

findings.detail: Compilation tools available on system
findings.evidence: Found: /usr/bin/gcc, /usr/bin/cc, /usr/bin/g++, /usr/bin/make, /usr/bin/as, /usr/bin/ld
findings.remediation: Remove compilers from production systems to impede exploit compilation

findings.detail: No cron access control files found
findings.evidence: Neither /etc/cron.allow nor /etc/cron.deny exists - all users can schedule cron jobs
findings.remediation: Create /etc/cron.allow with authorized users only

findings.detail: World-writable systemd service: /usr/lib/systemd/system/cryptdisks-early.service
findings.evidence: Permissions: Dcrw-rw-rw-
findings.remediation: chmod 0644 /usr/lib/systemd/system/cryptdisks-early.service

findings.detail: World-writable systemd service: /usr/lib/systemd/system/cryptdisks.service
findings.evidence: Permissions: Dcrw-rw-rw-
findings.remediation: chmod 0644 /usr/lib/systemd/system/cryptdisks.service

findings.remediation: Consider running the service with a dedicated non-root user

findings.detail: User is member of privileged group: root
findings.evidence: Root group membership may grant additional privileges
findings.remediation: Remove user from root group if not required: gpasswd -d $USER root

findings.detail: Writable sensitive proc file: /proc/sysrq-trigger
findings.evidence: Can potentially be used for container escape
findings.remediation: Use read-only /proc mount or restrict access with AppArmor/SELinux

findings.detail: Current user: root (UID: 0, GID: 0)
findings.evidence: uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)

findings.detail: Already running as root (UID 0)
findings.evidence: No privilege escalation needed - already at maximum privilege level

findings.detail: Current user can read /etc/shadow
findings.evidence: Permissions: -rw-r----- | Password hashes are exposed
findings.remediation: chmod 0640 /etc/shadow; chown root:shadow /etc/shadow

findings.detail: Weak or legacy password hash for user: systemd-network
findings.evidence: Password may use DES or other weak hashing algorithm
findings.remediation: Force password change for systemd-network with a modern algorithm

findings.detail: Weak or legacy password hash for user: polkitd
findings.evidence: Password may use DES or other weak hashing algorithm
findings.remediation: Force password change for polkitd with a modern algorithm

findings.detail: Readable sensitive file: /etc/fstab
findings.evidence: Filesystem table (may contain credentials for CIFS/NFS mounts)
findings.remediation: Restrict access to /etc/fstab

findings.detail: World/group-readable home directory: /home/claude
findings.evidence: Permissions: drwxr-xr-x
findings.remediation: chmod 0750 /home/claude
